commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh She makes fine linen, and sells it; and delivers sashes unto the merchants. The Chanaanite, the merchant; for Chanaanite, in Hebrew, signifies a merchant. (Challoner) The Phoenicians travelled into all countries. Traffic was not then deemed a discredit, even to kings. Girdles were worn both by men and women, and were very costly, insomuch that (Calmet) the kings of Persia assigned cities to furnish their wives with them. ) They who practise and teach the law may be said to buy and sell. (Worthington).

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: The Chanaanite: The merchant, for Chanaanite, in Hebrew, signifies a merchant.
